{{Quote|The Leafar have begun to challenge the Empire and are certain to face retribution. Alliance forces are therefore being sent to protect an important transfer point near their system.|Alliance officer|<ref name="X-wing" />}}
The '''Leafar''' were a group involved in [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] shipping based out of the [[Leafar system/Legends|Leafar system]]. Shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], they began to challenge the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The Empire responded by sending the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Badi Dea]]'' to [[Imperial ambush of the Leafar|attack]] their shipping convoys. The ambush was foiled by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], which, initially, sent five [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighters]] to protect Leafar shipping. Three separate groups of Leafar [[Starship/Legends|ships]] were defended by the Alliance, which called in reinforcements as the battle continued. The Leafar later aided the Alliance by supplying it with information on a new [[Shield X|shield]] technology being produced by the Empire.

==History==
{{Quote|If we can protect their shipping from Imperial attack we will have gained a new ally.|Alliance officer|<ref name="X-wing" />}}
[[File:Leafar.png|thumb|right|300px|Keyan Farlander's T-65 X-wing starfighter pursues a TIE/Advanced starfighter near a Leafar convoy.]]
The Leafar were a group who controlled<ref name="X-wing" /> the [[Leafar system/Legends|Leafar system]], located in the [[Greater Plooriod Cluster]] of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="TEAOC" /> They were involved in [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] shipping. Shorty after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destruction]] of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]] over [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], they began to actively challenge the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. In retribution, the Empire dispatched the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Badi Dea]]'' to an important Leafar transfer point located near their system, with the aim of destroying numerous Leafar merchant vessels. Unaware of the Imperial presence, a Leafar convoy consisting of five passenger-carrying [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvettes]] from the ''[[Yavin Express]]'' group, three goods-carrying [[BFF-1 bulk freighter/Legends|BFF-1 bulk freighters]] from the ''[[Cargo-Go]]'' group, and seven [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttles]] from the [[Lambda group|''Lambda'' group]] began to emerge from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] at the transfer point.<ref name="X-wing" />

Rather than the Imperial forces waiting in ambush, however, the Leafar witnessed a [[Imperial ambush of the Leafar|battle]] already underway: five [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] from the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] were [[Starfighter combat/Legends|engaging]] [[TIE/Advanced starfighter]]s and [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]] from the ''Badi Dea''.<ref name="Guide">''[[X-Wing Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide]]''</ref> The [[Alliance High Command/Legends|leadership]] of the Rebel Alliance had heard of the Leafar's growing insurgency, and, hoping to gain a new ally, they had dispatched [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]] and four other [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] from [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]] to the Leafar way-point.<ref name="X-wing" /> With the arrival of the Leafar convoy, the Imperial [[Bomber/Legends|bombers]] began targeting the merchant [[Starship/Legends|ships]], prompting Farlander to focus on destroying the bombers, while his [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmates]] held off the TIE Advanced.<ref name="Guide" />

Given the significant Imperial presence, the Rebels brought in reinforcements, with two [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptors]] from [[Gold Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Gold Squadron]] arriving several minutes into the engagement.<ref name="Guide" /> Thirteen minutes later,<ref name="X-wing" /> the [[MC80 Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 Star Cruiser]] [[Maria (MC80 Star Cruiser)|''Maria'']] and the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Whitehawk]]'' arrived, launching additional [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]. The combined Rebel forces were able to hold off several waves of Imperial starfighters long enough for the majority of the Leafar convoy to escape, before disengaging themselves.<ref name="Guide" /> Of the total Leafar ships, at least three [[Corvette/Legends|corvettes]], two [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]], and four [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttles]] were able to escape the ambush.<ref name="X-wing" />

Following this assault, the Leafar were able to supply the Rebel Alliance with some intelligence that they had gained on Imperial plans. The Alliance had captured several TIE/Advanced starfighters with a new [[Shield X|shield technology]], and the Leafar knew this was being developed for use with larger starships, such as [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] corvettes. Following receipt of this information, the Alliance eventually managed to capture the prototype corvette [[Ram's Head|''Blade 5'']], using it to ram the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridges]] of numerous [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] as part of [[Operation Ram's Head]].<ref name="X-wing" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Leafar were created for ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Tour of Duty: Imperial Pursuit]]'', the first expansion pack for the award-winning [[Star Wars video games|computer game]] [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']], created by [[LucasArts]] and released in [[1994]]. The Leafar were connected with two missions carried out by the player in the role of Keyan Farlander. In the first, their convoy is under attack, while in the second, they are only mentioned in its framing story.<ref name="X-wing" />

The ''[[X-Wing Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide]]'', published in [[1995]] by [[Prima Games]], provided players with walkthroughs of the relevant missions, presented as Farlander's mission reports. Consequently, while it is in theory possible for a player to complete the mission prior to the arrival of the ''Maria'' and ''Whitehawk'', [[canon]]ically Farlander did not.<ref name="Guide" /> There is no canonical number of Leafar starships that survived, but there is a minimum of nine—three corvettes, two freighters, and four shuttles—set out for the mission to be successful.<ref name="X-wing" />

While the Leafar control a [[Star/Legends|star]] system and are an entity capable of joining the Rebel Alliance, it is not made clear whether they are a [[Species/Legends|species]], a culture, or an organization. Until canon clarification, this article treats them as a generic organization.
